definition: Per-relation domain-violation score D = m/(m+1) in [0,1), where m is a dimensionless, relation-specific measure of precondition violation computed from already-committed artifacts only; see m_definition per entry.

relations.m_definition: m = 0 by construction: a node permutation is an exact relabeling with no geometric/physical precondition to violate; the follow-up input is exactly samplable (bit-identical coordinates).
relations.honesty_boundary: Construction argument; the committed pilot ledger records the executed relation (measured violation 0.0 <= 1e-6) but D = 0 does not depend on the measurement.

relations.m_definition: m = worst reflection involution offset (committed symmetric-mesh ledger, ~machine epsilon) over the real-mesh median edge length, the common length scale used by the existing single-relation script.
relations.honesty_boundary: Geometry-only score; same definition as the committed single-relation report (low D: a violation here reads as SUT inconsistency).

relations.m_definition: m = worst reflected-node nearest-existing-node distance in median-edge-length units (committed precondition report).
relations.honesty_boundary: Geometry-only score; same definition and value as the committed single-relation report (high D: out-of-relation-domain / OOD-stress).

relations.m_definition: m = mean nondimensional discrete-divergence imbalance of the committed reference field on the deployed open-boundary mesh (mean_reference_nondim_divergence): the open-boundary flux-imbalance relative quantity, i.e. how far the deployed data sit from the divergence-free validity domain of the absolute relation.
relations.honesty_boundary: D quantifies only the domain-side precondition imbalance. The executed verdict for this MR was DEFERRED through the numerical-decidability gate (the absolute tolerance 1e-6 is uncalibratable: imbalance/tol ~ 3.7e4), which is the tolerance axis of the verdict plane, not the domain axis. No absolute conservation claim is made or implied.

relations.m_definition: m = 0 by construction: the committed sweep evaluates the P1 divergence operator on an analytic stream-function field with div(u) = 0 exactly, on a mesh family symmetric by construction, so the precondition is satisfied exactly.
relations.honesty_boundary: Calibration diagnostic feeding the admissibility predicate, not a verdict-bearing MR execution; D = 0 states only that its reference field is in-domain by construction.

relations.m_definition: m = 0 by construction: pointwise MLP evaluation is row-independent, so permuting evaluation points and inverting is an exact relabeling with no precondition to violate (relation vacuous by construction, C14).
relations.honesty_boundary: Construction argument; committed K=6 measured violations (max 6.67e-09) are well-definedness checks only and carry no evidential weight.

relations.m_definition: m = 0 by construction: the closed-form domain [-1,1]^2 is exactly invariant under the mirror and reflected evaluation points are computed in closed form (no mesh, no interpolation), so the reflected-point placement error (the same m as the MGN mirror-y score) is exactly zero; the committed SUT manifest records has_mirror_symmetry = true.
relations.honesty_boundary: With D = 0, a measured MR-B failure (heat seeds s1/s2 in the K=6 roster) reads as SUT inconsistency on the verdict plane, subject to C14's caveat that the floor is the PINN's self-reported PDE residual, not an independently calibrated floor.

relations.m_definition: m = 0 by construction: the closed-form domain [-1,1]^2 is exactly invariant under the mirror and reflected evaluation points are computed in closed form (no mesh, no interpolation), so the reflected-point placement error (the same m as the MGN mirror-y score) is exactly zero; the committed SUT manifest records has_mirror_symmetry = true.
relations.honesty_boundary: With D = 0, a measured MR-B failure (heat seeds s1/s2 in the K=6 roster) reads as SUT inconsistency on the verdict plane, subject to C14's caveat that the floor is the PINN's self-reported PDE residual, not an independently calibrated floor.

relations.m_definition: m = max_t |Q_ref(t) - Q_ref(0)| / |Q_ref(0)| over the committed per-snapshot reference table (Q = integral of u_x, Dirichlet zero BC): the open-boundary flux-imbalance relative quantity of the reference itself.
relations.honesty_boundary: Nonzero m is exactly why the executed MR is reference-relative rather than absolute; D quantifies the distance of the deployed case from an exact-conservation validity domain.

relations.m_definition: m = max_t |Q_ref(t) - Q_ref(0)| / |Q_ref(0)| over the committed per-snapshot reference table (Q = integral of u, Neumann zero-flux BC): the committed reference conserves Q bit-identically, so m = 0.
relations.honesty_boundary: Exact-conservation precondition holds on the committed reference (zero-flux boundary); a conservation violation here would read as SUT inconsistency.

relations.m_definition: A per-K=6-seed D would need per-snapshot Q_ref tables, which the committed K=6 roster reports do not contain (they commit only median conservation ratios).
relations.honesty_boundary: Not operationalizable from committed data; no number is invented. The class-level D for PINN MR-C comes from the cross-family pilot artifacts (entries pinn-mr-c-conservation-burgers / -heat).
relations.reason: pinn-k6-roster/*/mr_report.json commit only mr_c_median_ratio; no per-snapshot Q_ref is committed.

honesty_boundary: Per-relation operationalization from committed measurements only. The per-relation m's use different relation-specific units, so the D values are NOT mutually calibrated across MR classes or domains; no cross-domain calibrated or validated domain-violation metric is claimed. Entries marked exact-by-construction rest on construction arguments recorded alongside committed artifacts; the one entry without committed support is explicitly marked not-operationalizable-from-committed-data.
